req.title: Manager creates leave-request
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a leave-request through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct risk zones: (1) happy-path creation by an authenticated manager, (2) validation rejection when mandatory fields are absent, and (3) boundary behaviour on field length limits. A fourth test covers the authentication boundary (unauthenticated actor). Together these tests exercise EP (valid partition), NEG (missing fields, wrong role, no auth), BVA (max-length field value), and STC (unauthenticated → authenticated state transition). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own state.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_leave_request_successfully
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Capture the created entity ID from the response / page
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity ID
Audit record exists for the created leave-request
trace: R-WEB-013: positive path – authenticated manager, all mandatory fields present
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_creates_leave_request_successfully(
    manager_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for role:manager
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for manager
    audit_helper,        # <helper_fn>: checks audit log via API
    leave_request_cleanup,  # <fixture>: deletes created entity after test
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests")
    page.get_by_label("Start Date").fill("2025-09-01")
    page.get_by_label("End Date").fill("2025-09-05")
    page.get_by_label("Reason").fill("Annual leave")
    page.get_by_label("Type").select_option("ANNUAL")
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/leave-requests" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    leave_request_cleanup(entity_id)
    assert audit_helper.record_exists(
        action="CREATE", resource="leave-request", resource_id=entity_id
    ), "Audit record not found for created leave-request"

tests.name: test_create_leave_request_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
expected: Form submission is rejected (HTTP 400 or browser-level validation prevents POST)
At least one inline validation error message is visible on the page
trace: R-WEB-013: error pathway – 400 on validation error; mandatory fields missing
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_create_leave_request_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(
    manager_page,    # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for role:manager
    audit_helper,    # <helper_fn>: checks audit log via API
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests")
    # Deliberately skip filling any mandatory field
    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r)
            if "/leave-requests" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST" else None)
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(500)  # allow any async validation to settle
    # Either a 400 was returned or the browser blocked submission entirely
    api_responses = [r for r in responses if r.request.method == "POST"]
    if api_responses:
        assert api_responses[0].status == 400, (
            f"Expected 400, got {api_responses[0].status}"
        )
    # Validation messages must be visible
    error_locator = page.locator("[role='alert'], .field-error, [aria-invalid='true']")
    expect(error_locator.first).to_be_visible()
    # Confirm no audit record was written for an anonymous/empty resource
    assert not audit_helper.any_recent_create(
        action="CREATE", resource="leave-request", window_seconds=5
    ), "Audit record must not be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_create_leave_request_reason_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Maximum allowed length for the Reason field is known (assumed 255 characters)
actions: Fill all mandatory fields; set Reason to exactly 255 characters
expected: Leave-request is created with the full 255-character reason preserved
trace: R-WEB-013: boundary – maximum allowed field length for 'Reason' (255 chars)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_REASON_LEN = 255

def test_create_leave_request_reason_at_max_length_boundary(
    manager_page,
    audit_helper,
    leave_request_cleanup,
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/leave-requests")
    boundary_reason = "A" * MAX_REASON_LEN
    page.get_by_label("Start Date").fill("2025-10-01")
    page.get_by_label("End Date").fill("2025-10-03")
    page.get_by_label("Reason").fill(boundary_reason)
    page.get_by_label("Type").select_option("ANNUAL")
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/leave-requests" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    assert body.get("reason") == boundary_reason, "255-char reason not persisted verbatim"
    leave_request_cleanup(entity_id)
    assert audit_helper.record_exists(
        action="CREATE", resource="leave-request", resource_id=entity_id
    ), "Audit record not found for boundary-length leave-request"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_leave_request
preconditions: No active authenticated session exists (cookies cleared)
actions: Open a fresh browser context with no session cookies
Attempt to POST a well-formed leave-request payload via API
expected: UI redirects to login page OR displays 401/403 error
Direct API POST to /leave-requests returns HTTP 401
trace: R-WEB-013: error pathway – 401 if unauthenticated; STC from unauthenticated state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"  # <fixture>: replace with env-var or config fixture

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_leave_request(
    browser,         # <fixture>: Playwright Browser instance (no session)
    audit_helper,    # <helper_fn>: checks audit log via API
):
    # --- UI layer: must redirect to login ---
    context = browser.new_context()  # fresh context; no stored auth state
    page = context.new_page()
    page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/leave-requests")
    # Expect redirect to login or a 401/403 page indicator
    assert "/login" in page.url or page.get_by_text("401").is_visible() \
        or page.get_by_text("Unauthorized").is_visible(), (
        f"Unauthenticated user should not reach /leave-requests; landed at {page.url}"
    )
    context.close()
    # --- API layer: raw POST without auth token must return 401 ---
    payload = {
        "startDate": "2025-11-01",
        "endDate": "2025-11-05",
        "reason": "test-unauth",
        "type": "ANNUAL",
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/leave-requests", json=payload, timeout=10)
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert not audit_helper.any_recent_create(
        action="CREATE", resource="leave-request", window_seconds=5
    ), "Audit record must not be written for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_role_forbidden_employee_cannot_create_leave_request
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (NOT manager)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role:employee
POST a well-formed leave-request payload directly to /leave-requests API
trace: R-WEB-013: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden; DT covers role=employee column
